- Bar: blue at cols 36-39, gray at cols 36-38

- Bar stayed at cols 36-39 (didn't shrink because white block expanded LEFT, away from bar)

I need to get the white block on the RIGHT side of the player, adjacent to the bar. To do that, I need to move RIGHT so the white block is on the right side.

- Bar: at cols 36-39
If I move RIGHT 1:
- Player: green at cols 34-36, white at col 37 (white on right side, inside bar at 36-39)
Hmm, the white block would be inside the bar. Let me try moving RIGHT and then SPACE to see if the white block expands RIGHT into the bar.
Actually, wait. Let me think about this more carefully. The white block is always on the side the player last moved. If I move RIGHT, the white block is on the right side. If I then press SPACE, the white block expands RIGHT.

Move RIGHT 1: green at 34-36, white at 37 (right side)
SPACE: white expands RIGHT from col 37 to... where? The bar is at 36-39, so the white block would expand into the bar.

Goal hypothesis: Destroy the bar by eating it with white block.
Plan: Move RIGHT so white block is on right side, adjacent to bar, then SPACE to eat bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First shrink white block by pressing SPACE
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"After SPACE (shrink): reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Move RIGHT so white block is on right side
result = action(['RIGHT'])
print(f"\nAfter RIGHT: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check bar
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['g', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) >= 28 and max(rows) <= 31 and min(cols) >= 30 and max(cols) <= 45:
            print(f"  Bar obj: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Now SPACE to expand white block RIGHT into bar
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE (expand RIGHT): reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 24 and max(rows) <= 40:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check bar
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['g', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if min(rows) >= 28 and max(rows) <= 31 and min(cols) >= 30 and max(cols) <= 45:
            print(f"  Bar obj: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
